text: The surgeon completes the laparoscopic mobilization of the terminal ileum and right colon using a medial to lateral approach, followed by the takedown of the hepatic flexure.
The surgeon performs a four-centimeter peri-umbilical incision and inserts a wound protector to facilitate the extraction of the specimen.

The ultrasound probe is covered with a sterile probe cover, and the small bowel is irrigated with sterile saline using a bladder syringe to facilitate acoustic coupling for intraoperative ultrasound assessment.
The surgeon demonstrates thickening of the mucosa and submucosa in the terminal ileum and ileocecal region, visible through intraoperative ultrasound imaging.
The intraoperative ultrasound reveals skip lesions in the small bowel, which are visible as areas of normal bowel interspersed with diseased segments.
The ultrasound image displays a comparison between normal small bowel at the top, showing typical thin walls, and inflamed, thickened small bowel at the bottom, with visibly increased wall thickness.

The surgical field shows a limited ileocecal resection with a side-to-side ileocolic anastomosis. Three Heineken-Mikulitz strictureplasties are performed, demonstrating the surgical technique used to address bowel strictures.
